Good Health and Well-being.reason: Patent law is fundamental for protecting R&D in medicines and vaccines (Target 3.b). The course teaches the legal framework applicable to such health-related innovations.

Quality Education.reason: As a graduate-level law course, it provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and specialized 'Employability skills' (Target 4.4) for the legal and technology sectors.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly supports economic growth through technological upgrading and innovation (Target 8.2) by teaching how to legally protect inventions and creative work.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course is fundamentally about the legal framework for patent law, which is designed to 'encourage innovation' and support 'technology development' (Targets 9.5, 9.b).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: A case study on '土木工程技術' (civil engineering technology) has a minimal, tangential link to infrastructure development in cities (Target 11.c).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Patent law protects new technologies, which can include environmentally sound technologies that support sustainable production patterns (Target 12.a), an inferred application of course content.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is an in-depth study of '專利法' (Patent Law) and '法院判決' (court cases), directly promoting the rule of law and strong institutions (Targets 16.3, 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Patent law is a key international system governing access to technology and innovation (Target 17.6). The course provides foundational knowledge for this global mechanism.
